body.title: CLOSED: Logging primitive for engram execution
body.result: engram_log + engram_execution_context; wired push_sources×2 + main.py; 4 tests; commit d1159fc5
body.description:
Engrams (procedural memories / habits) currently execute as black boxes — no log output during execution. This means stepping through cognition or auditing habit behavior has no instrumentation at the engram level. When stepping catches a missing data point, there's no way to add a log call from inside an engram's code_ref.

Add a log primitive callable from inside engram execution:
- `engram_log(message, level='info')` — emits a line to pe_chain.log (or a dedicated engram.log) tagged with the engram id and execution context
- Available in the engram execution environment (wherever code_ref functions run)
- Captured in turn_trace so /cognition-debug stepping can show engram log lines alongside phase outputs

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/cognition/pe_chain.py or wherever code_ref is invoked, wild_igor/igor/tools/registry.py (expose engram_log to registered tools)
**Design rules:** docs-in-code. logging-as-debug-base (feedback rule: log what would have pointed at the bug immediately).
**Scope boundary:** IN: engram_log primitive + wiring into execution context + turn_trace capture. OUT: adding log calls to existing engrams (that's a follow-on pass), modifying the engram schema.
**Test plan:** register a test engram with an engram_log() call; fire it; verify the log line appears in pe_chain.log and in the turn_trace for that execution.
